title: Area of Triangles
why: A triangle is exactly half of a rectangle (or parallelogram) with the same base and height. So triangle area is base × height, then take half. The feeder skills are rectangle area and halving a number — get them warm and the triangle formula feels obvious.

diagnostic.q: A rectangle is 8 by 5. What is its area?

learn.heading: A triangle is half a rectangle
learn.intro: Take any rectangle and cut it along a diagonal — you get two equal triangles. So a triangle's area is base × height, then divided by 2 (take half). First find base × height like a rectangle, then halve it.
examples: Rectangle 6 × 4 = 24. A triangle with the same base and height has area = half of 24 = 12 square units.
The height is straight up from the base (perpendicular), not the slanted side of the triangle.

tiers.intro: Multiply, then halve — one step at a time.

items.q: B. A triangle is half of that. Half of 24 = ___?
C. A triangle has a slanted side of 7 and a straight-up height of 5. Which number is the height?
items.hint: Height is perpendicular (straight up), so it is 5.

items.q: A. A triangle has base 10 and height 6. Find base × height ÷ 2: ___?
B. A triangle has base 9 and height 4. What is its area?

exit.q: 2. A triangle has base 8 and height 5. Find base × height ÷ 2: ___?
